one_time_description: Settlement agreement reached May 7, 2024 with contractor related to Nicknaqueet River Mini Hydro Project and Youth Centre Project; Nation to pay $1,000,000
economic_notes: Nation holds investments in Kvamua Enterprises Limited Partnership (100%), Central Coast Commercial Fishing Limited Partnership (25%), Central Coast Commercial Fisheries Corporation (25%), Coastal Nations Fisheries Limited Partnership (12.50%). Also holds portfolio investments in Wuikinuxv Economic Development Corporation, Great Bear Carbon Credit Corporation, Great Bear Carbon Credit Limited Partnership, BC First Nations Gaming LP, Coastal Nations Fisheries (GP) Inc. Midawis Maintenance Services Limited Partnership is consolidated as a controlled entity. Nation is a beneficiary of Coastal Nations Fisheries Trust.
notes: Fiscal year is 2023 (year ended March 31, 2023), not 2024. Financial statements signed December 16, 2024 indicating late filing. Three GBEs accounted for under modified equity method: Kvamua Enterprises LP (100%), Central Coast Commercial Fisheries LP (25%), Coastal Nations Fisheries LP (12.50%). One controlled entity consolidated line-by-line: Midawis Maintenance Services LP. Prior year revenue of $37.9M was significantly higher due to large Provincial transfers ($10.4M vs $1.5M) and large GBE earnings ($12.2M vs $2.0M). Nation adopted PS 3280 Asset Retirement Obligations and PS 3450 Financial Instruments prospectively on April 1, 2022. Liability for contaminated sites of $3,882,222 and new asset retirement obligations of $2,008,636 on balance sheet. Post-balance sheet settlement of $1,000,000 with contractor. Total government transfers calculated as ISC $2,340,865 + Province of BC $1,461,246 + FNHA $1,125,003 + DFO $1,170,339 + CMHC $273,506 + CIRNAC $32,322 + CCIRA $385,410 + Great Bear Initiative Society $230,275 + Coast Opportunity Fund $336,172 (some may be non-government) - used identifiable government sources only for total_government_transfers. OSR calculated as total revenue minus identified government transfers.
